news.headline: Caribou Biosciences added to Russell 2000 and Russell Microcap indices
news.summary: CRBU was added to the reconstituted Russell 2000, Russell Microcap and related value benchmark indices at the June 2026 rebalance. Index inclusion brings incremental passive fund ownership, a modest structural tailwind for this thinly traded micro-cap. Management also presented at the H.C. Wainwright Cell Therapy virtual conference the same week.

news.headline: Caribou shareholders reject officer exculpation proposal at annual meeting
news.summary: At its 2026 annual meeting Caribou shareholders elected the director slate and ratified the auditor but voted down a proposed charter amendment extending liability exculpation to corporate officers. A governance signal reflecting some investor pushback rather than a fundamental driver.

news.headline: Caribou's off-the-shelf CAR-T drives durable myeloma responses (CB-011)
news.summary: Feature coverage of the CaMMouflage trial for allogeneic anti-BCMA CB-011 highlighted deep, durable responses in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. Combined with the FDA RMAT designation granted in March 2026, the data strengthen the case that Caribou's CRISPR-edited, off-the-shelf CAR-T can rival autologous cell therapies.

news.headline: Caribou reports long-term vispa-cel (CB-010) data in second-line large B-cell lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: Updated ANTLER data for vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) showed roughly 82% ORR, 67% CR and a 17.1-month median PFS in optimized-dose second-line LBCL patients, with no GvHD and minimal severe CRS. The durability underpins Caribou's argument that its allogeneic product can match approved autologous CAR-T ahead of the planned ANTLER-3 pivotal Phase 3.

news.headline: Caribou reports CB-011 dose-escalation durability data at EHA 2026
news.summary: CB-011 (anti-BCMA) posted a roughly 92% ORR, 75% or greater CR and 91% MRD negativity in BCMA-naive myeloma patients at the recommended expansion dose, with durable complete responses. The clean efficacy and safety profile positions the RMAT-designated program as pivotal-ready and is Caribou's most advanced myeloma catalyst.

news.headline: Citi maintains Buy rating on Caribou Biosciences with $8 price target
news.summary: Citi reiterated a Buy rating and $8 price target following the EHA 2026 readouts, implying multi-fold upside versus the sub-$3 share price. The reaffirmation reflects growing sell-side confidence in the vispa-cel and CB-011 datasets, though the target embeds successful financing of the pivotal program.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings & business update
catalysts.detail: Expected August 2026 report with updated cash position and, importantly, any commentary on financing the vispa-cel pivotal trial.

catalysts.label: vispa-cel ANTLER-3 pivotal Phase 3
catalysts.detail: Company is exploring funding options for the ~250-patient registrational trial in 2L LBCL (PFS primary endpoint); initiation is contingent on securing capital or a partner.

catalysts.label: CB-011 CaMMouflage expansion data
catalysts.detail: Additional durability and MRD-negativity data from the RMAT-designated anti-BCMA myeloma program could support an accelerated regulatory path.

catalysts.label: Cash runway limit
catalysts.detail: Roughly $118.6M cash as of Q1 2026 funds operations into the second half of 2027, making a financing or partnership a key near-term catalyst and overhang.

catalysts.label: Investor conference appearances
catalysts.detail: Management continues presenting at cell-therapy and healthcare conferences (e.g. H.C. Wainwright, Jefferies), which can support awareness and partnering discussions.

pulse: Sentiment is cautiously constructive after strong EHA 2026 readouts: vispa-cel (roughly 82% ORR, 17.1-month PFS in 2L LBCL) and CB-011 (roughly 92% ORR, 91% MRD negativity in myeloma) both suggest Caribou's off-the-shelf CRISPR CAR-T can rival autologous therapies, and CB-011 carries FDA RMAT designation. The sell side is bullish, with Citi reiterating Buy and an $8 target implying large upside versus the sub-$3, newly Russell-included micro-cap. The dominant overhang is financing: with about $118.6M and runway only into 2H 2027, the ~250-patient ANTLER-3 pivotal depends on raising capital or landing a partner, so dilution risk caps enthusiasm. Net setup is high-beta and binary, hinging on clinical follow-through and a funding solution over the next several quarters.
